test: remove text extraction test files.

fix: Integrate Auth with Stripe and Usage

- Added get_optional_current_user to backend
- Updated upload and usage endpoints to support authenticated users
- Updated frontend api.ts to inject Bearer token
- Ensures payments and usage are linked to the logged-in user
feat(subscriptions): integrate stripe checkout and fix user identity

feat: Backend Stripe Integration

- Created endpoint to generate checkout sessions
- Implemented webhook handler for plan upgrades
- Added Stripe configuration variables
- Registered new routers in main app

feat: Backend Stripe Integration

fix: Remove unused variable causing build failure

feat: Add usage visualization to frontend

- Added UsageBadge component to header
- Created useUsageStore for subscription state
- Integrated getUsage API endpoint
- Added visual indicators for remaining credits
- Wrapped upload error handling for limit reached

feat: Add Phase 1 subscription system - usage tracking

PHASE 1: BACKEND USAGE TRACKING

Implements freemium model with browser fingerprinting:
- Free tier: 5 PDFs/month (anonymous)
- Free with email: 10 PDFs/month
- Student: 50 PDFs/month
- Pro: Unlimited

NEW MODULES:
✓ backend/core/fingerprint.py - User identification via request headers
✓ backend/core/usage.py - Usage tracking with Redis + in-memory fallback (214 lines)
✓ backend/api/usage_routes.py - GET /api/usage endpoint

UPLOAD ENDPOINT CHANGES:
✓ Check limits BEFORE processing
✓ Return 429 when limit reached with helpful error
✓ Track usage AFTER successful upload
✓ Return remaining PDFs in response

DEPENDENCIES:
+ redis==5.0.1
+ stripe==7.10.0
+ python-jose==3.3.0

FEATURES:
- Plan-based limits (FREE_ANON, FREE_EMAIL, STUDENT, PRO)
- Monthly usage reset
- One-time credit top-ups
- Graceful Redis fallback to in-memory
- Comprehensive usage stats API

NEXT: Phase 2 - Stripe integration for paid subscriptions

fix: Re-enable TinyTextDetector with deduplication

PROBLEM: STAT202 detecting only 2/8 traps
ROOT CAUSE: TinyTextDetector was disabled

IMPLEMENTATION:
✓ Added deduplication logic to engine.py
  - Merges findings at same location (within 2px tolerance)
  - Keeps highest severity
  - Combines detector names (e.g. 'TinyTextDetector + MatchingColorDetector')

✓ Re-enabled TinyTextDetector
  - Changed enabled = False → True
  - Lowered min_text_length from 5 to 1 (catches single-char traps like 'z', 't', 'g')

✓ Fixed imports
  - Added Severity to engine.py for deduplication

EXPECTED RESULTS:
- STAT202: 6-8 findings (up from 2)
- No duplicate findings shown to users
- All tiny white text traps caught

Files changed:
- backend/core/engine.py: +43 lines (deduplication logic)
- backend/core/detectors/tiny_text.py: 2 changes
fix: Move toolbar to right side to avoid overlap
fix: Move toolbar below box to avoid blocking handles

feat: Add visual drag-to-resize for findings

Implemented interactive editing with drag handles!

NEW FEATURES:
✨ Click Edit (✏️) → Enter visual edit mode
✨ 8 drag handles (4 corners + 4 edges)
✨ Drag corners to resize both dimensions
✨ Drag edges to resize one dimension
✨ Drag center to move entire box
✨ Real-time coordinate updates
✨ Save/Cancel floating toolbar
✨ Keyboard shortcuts (Enter=Save, Escape=Cancel)

FILES CHANGED:
- frontend/src/components/ResizableBox.tsx (NEW)
  - Full drag logic for all 8 handles
  - Constrained to page boundaries
  - Hover states and cursor changes

- frontend/src/components/PDFViewer.tsx
  - Added editingFindingId prop
  - Render ResizableBox when editing
  - Pass onEditComplete/onEditCancel handlers

- frontend/src/App.tsx
  - Changed from modal editor to visual editor
  - Updated edit state management
  - Removed CoordEditor modal

Now users can precisely adjust finding boxes by dragging
directly on the PDF! Much more intuitive than typing numbers.

fix: Edited auto finding coordinates now properly sent to backend

CRITICAL BUG FIX:
- Track editedFindingIds in store
- Send edited auto findings as manual_regions with updated coords
- Backend now receives correct coordinates for edited findings

feat: Add coordinate editor for manual and auto findings

Allows users to edit x, y, width, height coordinates of any finding.

Frontend changes:
- Created CoordEditor modal component with number inputs
- Added updateFinding action to store for auto findings
- Added Edit button (✏️) to both FindingCard and ManualFindingCard
- Wired up App component with editingFinding state
- Handle save for both auto and manual findings

Users can now:
- Click Edit on any finding (auto or manual)
- Adjust coordinates via modal with validation
- See preview of coordinates before saving
- Fine-tune removal areas for maximum precision

feat: Add manual region selection feature (MVP)

Implements click-and-drag manual selection on PDF viewer for precise trap removal.

Frontend changes:
- Updated findingStore with ManualFinding interface and actions (add/remove/update)
- Added drawing canvas to PDFViewer with mouse events and coordinate conversion
- Added manual selection button to Sidebar with ManualFindingCard component
- Wired App component to manage drawing mode state
- Updated downloadSanitized API to send manual regions

Backend changes:
- Added ManualRegion model for API requests
- Updated SanitizeRequest to accept manual_regions parameter
- Extended sanitize endpoint to process manual regions as findings

Features:
- Users can click 'Add Manual Region' button
- Draw rectangles on PDF to select text regions
- Manual findings show in blue vs red for auto-detected
- Can delete/toggle manual findings like auto findings
- Manual regions sent to backend and sanitized correctly

This gives users ultimate control for edge cases where detectors
over-fire or miss traps.

fix: Disable TinyTextDetector to prevent duplicate findings

Root cause of normal text deletion diagnosed and fixed:
- MatchingColorDetector AND TinyTextDetector both flagged the same 1pt white text
- Created 2 findings instead of 1, causing confusion
- Normal text (12pt white bullet) was correctly skipped by both
- Issue was DUPLICATION not false positives

Solution: Disable TinyTextDetector since MatchingColorDetector already
handles detection of tiny white text.

Active detectors now (3 total):
- MatchingColorDetector: white/matching color text
- OffScreenTextDetector: off-screen positioned text
- ZeroWidthCharDetector: zero-width characters

Result: CS 171 now shows exactly 1 finding (hidden instruction)
Normal text will NOT be deleted.

fix: Disable aggressive detectors to eliminate false positives

Disabled 7 detectors that were causing excessive findings:
- SuspiciousSpacingDetector: too aggressive on normal spacing
- VisualMismatchDetector: computationally expensive, high FP rate
- LowContrastDetector: overlaps with MatchingColorDetector
- LayeredTextDetector: complex edge cases need tuning
- InvisibleRenderDetector: overlaps with VisualMismatchDetector
- HiddenAnnotationDetector: rarely useful for homework traps
- MetadataDetector: rarely useful for homework traps

Keeping enabled (conservative, tuned detectors):
- MatchingColorDetector: tuned with training data ✓
- TinyTextDetector: tuned with training data ✓
- OffScreenTextDetector: conservative, low FP rate
- ZeroWidthCharDetector: very specific, rare FPs

Expected result: CS 171 should show 1 finding (down from 13)
